title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A regional live-status operations team. They need bounded JSON and upload handling, correct problem responses, cancellation-safe streaming, explicit HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 configuration, TLS identity, timeouts, and graceful deploy behavior. Planned artifact: Deliver Resilient Protocol and Streaming Server with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.
Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
Stop stream producers on context cancellation, bound queues and bytes, preserve partial-response evidence, and avoid goroutine or resource leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Maxbytesreader Contentlength
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 7014 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. steps.title: Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Shutdown Close Register
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Shutdown Close Register: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project team handles case 7023 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Shutdown Close Register.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Shutdown Close Register, keep Shutdown Close Register connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Shutdown Close Register, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Server.Shutdown waits for hijacked connections and guarantees every cleanup callback has finished.
